text: The patient is positioned in the lithotomy position, with legs elevated and supported in stirrups, providing access to the anal region for the surgical procedure.

The surgeon makes three short incisions in the right thigh to harvest the gracilis muscle, extending to the tendon near the entry point of the anterior branch of the obturator nerve. The dissection is close to the vascular pedicle, with the terminal branch of the circumflex femoral artery visible approximately ten centimeters below the pubic tubercle.

The surgeon spreads the gracilis muscle forward to the perineal area, making an oval incision and wrapping it around the anus. An additional small incision is made below the tip of the coccyx, and the tendon of the gracilis muscle is fixed with interrupted sutures to the periosteum of the lower surface of the axial tuberosity on the same side.
